headline: Australia's CCUS ambitions undercut by policy gaps, comparative report finds
summary: A cross-country assessment ranks Australia's CCUS policy environment 'among the least enabling' of any high-income nation despite its self-styled leadership, flagging weaker incentives, demand-side support and permitting certainty than the US (45Q), EU and UK.
co2crc_note: Direct benchmark of Australia's policy settings against global peers — sharpens the case CO2CRC makes for stronger enabling frameworks around Safeguard/ACCU and offshore storage acreage.
extractor_note: Report author/publisher of the underlying comparative study not confirmed from the paywalled Carbon Pulse item; no financial dimension, commitment_status 'na'.

headline: Puro.earth certifies the world's first BECCS-from-biogas project, in Norway
summary: Inherit Carbon Solutions, with HoopCO2 and the Northern Lights JV, wins Europe's first BECCS certification under the Puro Standard — capturing biogenic CO2 from the VEAS wastewater plant, shipping liquefied CO2 and injecting it beneath the North Sea; more than 700 CORCs issued for the first four months' operation (Feb-May 2026).
co2crc_note: First verified biogenic-CO2 removal from biogas via ship-and-inject — a template linking waste-sector capture to marine CO2 transport and offshore storage MRV, all relevant to CO2CRC's storage and monitoring focus.
extractor_note: 700 CORCs are removal credits, not a money figure — amount null. Northern Lights JV (Equinor/Shell/TotalEnergies) tagged OG-major as the transport-storage partner.

headline: CO2CRC signs CCS R&D MoU with IIT Bombay as experts weigh lessons for scale-up
summary: Melbourne-based CO2CRC formalised bilateral R&D collaboration with IIT Bombay, India's centre of excellence for CCS, acknowledging Australia's research leadership; reported alongside a Melbourne forum where a leading US expert argued R&D will be decisive in scaling CCS safely and cost-effectively from demonstration to commercial deployment.
co2crc_note: Directly about CO2CRC — a new Australia-India CCS research channel and public positioning of CO2CRC's Bass Strait storage work for Latrobe Valley emitters.
extractor_note: Regional-newspaper feature; the concrete news is the CO2CRC-IIT Bombay MoU, placed in markets as a cross-border R&D partnership. Could equally sit under technology.

headline: Euroports and Normod Carbon sign MoU for a Northern European CO2 hub
summary: Belgian port operator Euroports and Norwegian CO2 aggregator Normod Carbon agreed a non-binding MoU to develop shared CO2 hub and shipping infrastructure across Northern Europe, extending Normod's aggregator model anchored by its planned EUR250m Port of Grenaa hub (up to 10 Mtpa, first operations 2029).
co2crc_note: Adds to the maturing liquefied-CO2 shipping and hub value chain that underpins cross-border storage models like Australia-Timor-Leste Bayu-Undan.
extractor_note: Non-binding MoU (announced). EUR250m / 10 Mtpa figures relate to Normod's separate Grenaa hub context, not this MoU — amount left null. Carbon Herald article slug inferred from headline; direct fetch blocked (403).

headline: Cemex wins EUR200m PERTE grant for SOMZERO carbon-capture project, Alcanar (Spain)
summary: Spain's PERTE industrial-decarbonisation programme awarded Cemex EUR200m toward SOMZERO, a >500 kt/yr cement carbon-capture project at Alcanar feeding a Catalonia CO2 hub; total investment exceeds EUR450m.
co2crc_note: Cement-sector capture funding model; example of a national grant mechanism (PERTE) de-risking hard-to-abate capture, of comparative interest for Australian industrial CCS policy.
extractor_note: Dated 17 Jul (out of the 21-22 Jul window) — carried as radar. EUR200m is a public allocation, not yet spent.

headline: Pertamina completes first water-injectivity test at Jatibarang (JTB-156), West Java
summary: PT Pertamina Drilling completed a first water-injectivity test at well JTB-156 in the Jatibarang field, an early reservoir-readiness milestone for the Indonesian NOC's CCS/CCUS pipeline under its new ICESS integrated service line.
co2crc_note: Under-covered NOC storage-characterisation work in a key APAC neighbour; reservoir-injectivity testing is directly analogous to CO2CRC's storage appraisal methods.
extractor_note: Reported ~20 Jul (test conducted 14 Jul) — out of window, carried as radar; from Shelly Murrell weekly monitoring. Petromindo article URL approximated.

headline: IEAGHG/CSIRO CO2-storage leakage review made openly accessible
summary: A detailed IEAGHG/CSIRO assessment of unlikely-but-potential CO2 migration to the surface concludes the probability of material environmental harm is very low, noting no confirmed leaks from commercial CCS to date; now made directly accessible via the IEAGHG website.
co2crc_note: Storage-integrity and social-licence evidence base directly usable in CO2CRC/Otway monitoring and stakeholder communications.
extractor_note: Carbon Herald article fresh (21 Jul) but the underlying IEAGHG/CSIRO report dates to Jan 2025 — carried as radar, not a fresh section item.
